interactions.summary: Jiang contrasts polytheism with the modern scientific-psychological worldview in which humans are memories, synapses, genes, and environment that can be managed to control anger, depression, and fate.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang; seg-0049 is a non-substantive fragment.

interactions.summary: Jiang asks why power would prefer the scientific-individual system and answers that it creates individual responsibility, harder work, and powerlessness by blocking collective action; a student begins to object that perspective-taking could still exist.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang with brief student confusion and objection.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_03 and SPEAKER_02 are students; the seg-0056 objection trails off and is answered in the next packet.

claims.claim: Jiang says the modern scientific worldview treats people as memories and synapses produced by genes and environment, promising self-management through therapy, reflection, and exercises.
He provocatively argues that polytheism is a more accurate reflection of reality than the modern neuroscience-psychology worldview.
Power prefers the scientific-individual worldview because it makes people easier to control, makes them work harder, and makes them blame themselves rather than society.
The second worldview creates powerlessness by preventing collective action: if problems are inside the individual, people cannot identify with others or change society together.

signature_moments.moment: Science is named as the modern religion, and Jiang says the older god-filled world explains reality better.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Now we live in a world where the religion is science.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The reversal converts a classroom comparison into a frontal attack on modern authority.

signature_moments.moment: The self-help world ends in isolation: no collective action, then video games or porn all day.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you're incapable of identifying with other people
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives Jiang's critique of individual psychology a social and political consequence.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The modern belief that problems originate inside the individual and can be fixed by managing memories, synapses, behavior, and self-control.
The capacity to identify with others and change the world together; Jiang says modern individualism disables it.

uncertainty_notes.note: The isolated 'Okay?' appears to be a diarization fragment and is not semantically important.
